_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to use data visualization tools (e.g., Flare, HighCharts, AmCharts, D3.js, Processing, Google Visualization API, Tableau, Raphael.js).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data visualization best practices, including chart type selection, cognitive load principles, color accessibility, and the difference between exploratory and explanatory visualization.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI AB domain. AB-K-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-A-001 (TF, cosine: 0.475), DA-K-004 (DA, cosine: 0.435).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.9/10 (raw 0.491). Shared vocabulary: [data, visualization]. DCWF-only terms [ability, amcharts, api, d3, flare, google] vs WIDAI-only terms [accessibility, best, chart, cognitive, color, difference] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.846 (P=0.838, R=0.854). Balanced P/R — the 17-word DCWF element and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.6%). "Ability to use data visualization tools (e.g., Flare, HighCharts, AmCharts, D3.j" and "Knowledge of data visualization best practices, including chart type selection," are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.632 > STS 0.491 (gap +0.142). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [data, visualization] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.4%. Shared tokens: [data, visualization]. DCWF-only: [ability, amcharts, api, d3, flare, google]. WIDAI-only: [accessibility, best, chart, cognitive, color, difference].

signals.detail: STS 0.491 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.
Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.142 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
